page: Amazon Web Services c6g.8xlarge is a compute-optimized server powered by the AWS Graviton processor family. It features 32 dedicated physical cores on the ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 architecture running at 2.5 GHz, with no simultaneous multithreading, providing exactly one thread per core. The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of memory, yielding a 2.0 GB per core ratio. It runs on the Nitro hypervisor, has a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps, and does not include local storage or GPU accelerators.

Benchmark results place the c6g.8xlarge in the average tier for most multi-core and processing tasks. The stress-ng multi-core CPU performance score is 48574.31, and the Geekbench multi-core score is 11649.0. Single-core performance is relatively weaker, with a Geekbench single-core score of 958.0 and a weak rating in single-threaded gzip compression at 20.33 GB/sec. However, multi-threaded operations like bzip3 decompression at 62.05 GB/sec and software compilation with Clang at 25603.0 perform in the average range.

Memory performance shows a contrast between a high cached read-only bandwidth of 1894.03 GB/sec and a weaker Passmark memory score of 1816.8. The server lacks local storage, meaning all storage must be network-attached. The hardware configuration and benchmark profile make this server suitable for multi-threaded workloads such as software compilation, static web serving, which achieved 33.12 GB/sec in testing, and database operations like Redis SET, which reached 38.64 million operations per second.
description: Amazon Web Services c6g.8xlarge is a compute-optimized server utilizing the ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 architecture with 32 dedicated physical cores running at 2.5 GHz. It features 64.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB per core, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor with 12 Gbps of baseline network bandwidth. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark data indicates average multi-core performance, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 11649.0 and a stress-ng multi-core score of 48574.31, though single-core metrics like Geekbench single-core are weaker. The server demonstrates average capabilities in multi-threaded tasks such as software compilation, static web serving at 33.12 GB/sec, and Redis database operations. It is suited for multi-threaded applications, web serving, and containerized microservices that benefit from dedicated physical ARM cores without hyperthreading.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6g.8xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized server with 32 dedicated Graviton Neoverse-N1 cores, 64 GB RAM, and 12 Gbps network bandwidth, suited for multi-threaded workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c6g.8xlarge is a compute-optimized ARM64 server featuring 32 dedicated physical cores, 64 GB RAM, and 12 Gbps network speed.
tagline: An ARM64 compute-optimized server featuring thirty-two dedicated physical cores and sixty-four gigabytes of memory for multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated physical ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 cores running at 2.5 GHz
64.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per core
12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Average multi-core performance in software compilation and static web serving
Suited for multi-threaded applications, web serving, and Redis database operations
